dropped_reasons.njs-atod2-overflow: maintainer flagged as harness bug, not an njs vulnerability

bugs.title: Decompression bomb causes OutOfMemoryError via unbounded codec decompression

bugs.review_notes: (some review notes were patch-related and may now be moot under description-only framing); Original PR #3625 was closed unmerged; actual fix landed via PR #3745 (AVRO-4247) on 2026-05-11 inside NonCopyingByteArrayOutputStream. Reporter URL points to the original (closed) PR.

harness.notes: Jazzer harness in PR body 'Trigger Method 2: Fuzzer (oss-fuzz / Jazzer)' section

bugs.title: Negative block size causes allocation-size-too-big crash

oss_fuzz.notes: Apache Avro OSS-Fuzz uses Jazzer with *Fuzzer.java targets discovered at build time; the specific Java harness exercising DataFileReader block-size validation is not directly identifiable from the public oss-fuzz project dir.

bugs.review_notes: Harness file used by FuzzingBrain to trigger this C-language Avro bug is not obvious from OSS-Fuzz integration (which targets the Java implementation). Bug is in [C] datafile reader per PR title; likely a custom harness.

harness.notes: libFuzzer harness in reporter's first comment on PR #3623

bugs.title: Negative string length causes allocation-size-too-big crash

oss_fuzz.notes: Bug is in [C] binary decoder per PR title; OSS-Fuzz Avro project is Java-only (Jazzer).

bugs.review_notes: Bug fix is in Avro C implementation; OSS-Fuzz integration covers Java only. Harness used by FuzzingBrain unclear.

harness.notes: libFuzzer harness in reporter's first comment on PR #3622

bugs.title: OOM in rust_demangle via unbounded lifetime count in demangle_binder

oss_fuzz.notes: rust_demangle is in libiberty/rust-demangle.c (shared with GCC). OSS-Fuzz binutils has many fuzz_*.c harnesses; fuzz_nm typically exercises symbol demangling.

bugs.review_notes: Sourceware bugzilla page is gated by Anubis (couldn't fetch directly); fix identified via GitHub mirror commit log referencing 'PR demangler/106641' which matches the bug description. Bugzilla 33878 report/disclosed date not retrievable. Harness file is a best-guess; could also be fuzz_objdump or fuzz_addr2line.

harness.notes: Sourceware bugzilla blocked by Anubis; harness pulled from cross-linked Ghidra advisory GHSA-m94m-fqr3-x442 which covers the shared libiberty/rust-demangle.c bug

bugs.title: Pre-Auth Memory Leak in FreeRDP NTLM AuthenticateMessage Parser Failure Path

oss_fuzz.notes: OSS-Fuzz FreeRDP uses cmake target 'fuzzers' to build all TestFuzz* binaries. FuzzingBrain's NTLM harness is in PR #12610 (not merged in this PR) - upstream OSS-Fuzz does not include an NTLM-specific fuzzer.

bugs.review_notes: (some review notes were patch-related and may now be moot under description-only framing); Fix landed in PR #12609 but the dedicated NTLM fuzzer (TestFuzzNTLM) was added separately in #12610 and is FuzzingBrain-authored; upstream OSS-Fuzz may not exercise NTLM message parsing directly.

harness.notes: Harness in PR #12610 (linked from final comment on issue #12603); source fetched from PR head sha c3c724726668e2ce112c2e4c638891aef78345a8

bugs.title: OOM in cplus_demangle due to malformed symbol

oss_fuzz.notes: Ghidra is not in OSS-Fuzz; the libFuzzer harness fuzz_rust_demangle.c shown in the advisory targets GPL/DemanglerGnu/src/demangler_gnu_v2_41/c/rust-demangle.c. Ghidra's 'fix' is adding a process-level timeout (not patching the demangler).

bugs.review_notes: Both ghidra-cplus-demangle-oom and ghidra-rust-demangle-oom are closed by the same advisory GHSA-m94m-fqr3-x442 and 'fixed' by the same commit 42e8ad7 (GP-6408: Demangler GNU analyzer timeout option), not by patching the underlying demangler code. The disclosed date is the advisory publish date.

harness.notes: Same advisory and same harness as ghidra-rust-demangle-oom; cplus_demangle is reached through the same demangler entry point

bugs.title: OOM in rust_demangle due to nested generic parameters

bugs.review_notes: (some review notes were patch-related and may now be moot under description-only framing); Same fix commit as ghidra-cplus-demangle-oom (process-level timeout, not a code patch).

bugs.title: StringIndexOutOfBoundsException in RegexLexer.consumeChar causes Internal Error

bugs.title: OOB Write in HarfBuzz fontations Glyph-Name Callback with size == 0

bugs.review_notes: Harness file best-guess (hb-shape-fuzzer.cc); could also be hb-subset-fuzzer.cc.

harness.notes: Only a small C main() reproducer is attached; no libFuzzer harness. Report says the bug was found by static data-flow audit, not by an existing fuzzer.

bugs.review_notes: ICU Jira (ICU-23365) is not publicly accessible without login - disclosed date unknown. Follow-up PR #3944 added a unit test and zero-init refactor (commit 10fc3f3f) - PR #3936 is the primary fix.

harness.notes: ICU Jira intermittently gated; standalone C++ reproducer extracted via WebFetch. OSS-Fuzz transliterator_fuzzer.cpp source not pasted in the Jira ticket.

bugs.title: Heap Buffer Overflow in libavif Android JNI via Signed-Int Length Sign-Extension

oss_fuzz.notes: Bug is in the Android JNI shim - not directly exercised by OSS-Fuzz which runs Linux libFuzzer; FuzzingBrain harness exercises the JNI entry points via a custom wrapper.

bugs.review_notes: Vulnerability is JNI-specific; OSS-Fuzz libavif targets the C API. Harness used by FuzzingBrain is custom.

harness.notes: Only a small Java JNI reproducer pasted; native binary 'tr1_neg_length_parse' referenced by name in confirmation command but source NOT attached. Needs follow-up for native harness.

bugs.title: Heap Buffer Overflow in mg_match

oss_fuzz.notes: OSS-Fuzz mongoose project ships test/fuzz.c and fuzz_netdriver_http.c; the issue's stack trace shows /src/mongoose/fuzz_security.c which is a FuzzingBrain-authored harness placed in the project root for OSS-Fuzz invocation.

bugs.review_notes: Reporter stack trace references fuzz_security.c at /src/mongoose - this is a FuzzingBrain custom harness, not one of upstream OSS-Fuzz's two harnesses (fuzz from test/fuzz.c, or fuzz_netdriver_http).

harness.notes: User-provided generic mg_match wrapper (split input → pattern + string). Substitutes for the original fuzz_security.c::fuzz_path_sanitization harness, which is not in any /data4/ze workspace. Exercises the same vulnerable function; site oracle still works since it counts library-code frames only. Existing 70-byte PoC may need regeneration for the new input scheme — TBD at Phase 2.

bugs.title: nDPI ndpi_hex_decode Unbounded sscanf Over Borrowed Buffer (TLS Block Parser)

oss_fuzz.notes: OSS-Fuzz nDPI uses ndpi/tests/ossfuzz.sh which builds many fuzzers (fuzz_alg_*, fuzz_dga, fuzz_protocols, etc.). The TLS block parser harness is likely fuzz_protocols.c or a TLS-specific target; PR 3163 added new harnesses too.

bugs.review_notes: (some review notes were patch-related and may now be moot under description-only framing); PR title 'add targeted parser harnesses' suggests the fix PR also added a new harness for this case (likely upstream-now). The triggering harness used by FuzzingBrain may have been custom prior to the fix.

harness.notes: Issue body names harness 'fuzz_ndpi_decode_tls_blocks'; source landed in PR #3163 (linked in comments); fetched from PR head

bugs.review_notes: OSS-Fuzz integration uses snmp_mib_fuzzer which targets MIB parsing; bug is in vacm_parse_config_group (config file parsing). Harness used by FuzzingBrain likely custom.

bugs.review_notes: Harness file is best-guess among three filestorage_read_* fuzzers.

harness.notes: Issue provides a small C++ driver. Notes that the bug was discovered by fixing OSS-Fuzz harness filestorage_read_file_fuzzer.cc (via google/oss-fuzz#15092); upstream OSS-Fuzz harness source not pasted in this issue.

bugs.title: Memory Leak in UPX PackLinuxElf32::pack2 when packing ELF executables

oss_fuzz.notes: OSS-Fuzz upx builds *_fuzzer.cpp targets from $SRC. FuzzingBrain authored a pack-file fuzzer (visible in issue body) targeting compression paths not covered by existing decompression fuzzers.

bugs.review_notes: (some review notes were patch-related and may now be moot under description-only framing); Maintainer closed issue with comment 'Done' but no commit was referenced. Memory leak is in ElfLinker::addLoader (linker.cpp). No matching commit found between 2025-12-08 and 2025-12-12 via git log or commit search. May be unfixed-but-marked-fixed, or fixed silently inside a large rebase commit.

harness.notes: Issue #945 references 'pack_file_fuzzer' by name; source pasted in sibling FuzzingBrain issue #947 (also upx)
